name: structured-live-update-doc-publishing
idea: Implement structured editable live-update documents for the TopicEditor. Current behavior: post_sectioned_topic stores a body plus loose sections and topic-level source_message_ids; render_topic emits one big Discord message with a Sources footer of raw message IDs; section media is dropped or model-copied into simple-topic media; publisher sends a single rendered string and trims at 2000 chars. Desired behavior: the agent creates a document-like draft in topics.summary with ordered blocks/sections. Each intro/section block carries its own source_message_ids and media_refs. Source citations render inline in brackets at the relevant block, not as an overall footer. Media refs bind by stable reference such as message_id plus attachment_index, not copied CDN URLs. Publisher resolves source IDs to Discord jump URLs using message metadata and resolves media refs to attachment/embed URLs at publish time. Publish order should be deterministic: header, intro text with inline linked sources, intro media, each section text with inline linked sources, that section media, one block at a time. Text chunking should be paragraph-aware only when a block exceeds Discord limits; normal sectioned topics should send one section per message. Add validation so media_refs must resolve to source messages/attachments and unknown refs do not silently disappear. Preserve existing topic_sources storage as the union of all block-level source IDs for audit/search. Update prompts/tool schemas so the agent is conscientious: every factual block gets its own sources; media is attached only to the relevant block; no global source footer. Keep backwards compatibility for old summaries with body/sections/source_message_ids as much as reasonable. Add focused tests covering section-level sources, media refs sent after relevant sections, no footer, source link hydration, chunking, and existing simple topic behavior.

overrides.note: Additional formatting requirement from user: rendered Discord output should use Markdown hierarchy. The full live-update/news item header should render as a level-2 Markdown header, e.g. '## Update: Kijai Assesses HiDream-O1-Image...'. Section/sub-point headers should render as bold text, e.g. '**Dev Model vs. Base Model**', followed by that section body with inline bracketed source links and section-owned media. Do not use one flat bold title plus plain section headers for structured documents.

notes.note: Additional formatting requirement from user: rendered Discord output should use Markdown hierarchy. The full live-update/news item header should render as a level-2 Markdown header, e.g. '## Update: Kijai Assesses HiDream-O1-Image...'. Section/sub-point headers should render as bold text, e.g. '**Dev Model vs. Base Model**', followed by that section body with inline bracketed source links and section-owned media. Do not use one flat bold title plus plain section headers for structured documents.

last_gate.rationale: Plan v2 resolves all six blocking flags. The two remaining 'open' flags (correctness, FLAG-005) both concern the 10-message cap on `get_topic_editor_message_context`. Plan Step 3 explicitly specifies a new method `get_topic_editor_source_messages(..., limit=50)` that returns `guild_id`, `attachments`, and `embeds` — it does not reuse the capped path. The four 'addressed' flags (issue_hints, scope, all_locations, callers) were resolved in v2 by adding the canonical `{kind, index}` media-ref schema, requiring compact `media_refs_available` in read-tool output, relaxing the missing-sections guard, and preserving `understand_media` shorthand conventions. Score trajectory improved (12.5 → 8.5), plan delta is substantial (83.5%), no recurring critiques from this plan's own flags, and preflight checks pass. Execution should proceed.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Weighted score improved from 12.5 to 8.5 after plan v2 addressed 4 of 6 significant flags. The two remaining open flags (correctness, FLAG-005) share the same root concern about a 10-message cap, which plan v2 explicitly resolves with `limit=50` on a new resolver method. No flag group shows the reopened-across-iterations pattern that would trigger TIEBREAKER. Preflight posture is clean (project dir exists, writable, criteria present, both Claude and Codex available).
warnings: Plan question remains open: should new structured writes store only `blocks` or preserve legacy `body`/`sections` alongside? Settle during implementation — `blocks`-only is cleaner but preserving both fields costs little and aids rollback.

The `topic_sources` union derivation in Step 6.1 uses merged current-window + archive rows. Ensure the implementation does not silently truncate source IDs beyond the archive resolver's limit (50). If a structured document legitimately exceeds 50 distinct source IDs, either raise the limit or explicitly reject with a clear error rather than silently omitting sources.
settled_decisions: Canonical media refs use `{message_id, kind: 'attachment'|'embed', index}`. Shorthand `{message_id, attachment_index}` normalizes to `{kind: 'attachment', index: attachment_index}` for backwards compatibility with `understand_media` tool.

Structured topics use `summary.blocks` as canonical storage. Legacy `body`/`sections`/`source_message_ids` are normalized into blocks at render/publish time but legacy simple-topic rendering behavior is preserved when `summary.blocks` is absent.
Archive-resolved source message rows feed all of: validation, source-author derivation, collision detection, citation link hydration, and media URL hydration — using one resolver to prevent drift.
Paragraph-aware chunking splits only when a single block exceeds Discord's limit; normal sectioned topics send one message per block.
last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
